content.text: Answer seven problems. (If you turn in more, the first seven will be graded.) Within reason, you may quote theorems as long as you state them clearly.

problem.text: Let \(K\) be a field and let \(f \in K[x]\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: State precisely a theorem concerning the existence and uniqueness of splitting fields for \(f\) over \(K\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that for any prime power \(p^n\), there is, up to isomorphism, a unique field of order \(p^n\).

problem.text: Let \(F\) be a normal extension of \(K\) and \(f \in K[x]\) an irreducible polynomial. Prove that in a factorization of \(f\) in \(F[x]\) the irreducible factors all have the same degree.
Let \(F\) be a free group on the set \(X\). Show that the automorphism group of \(F\) has a subgroup isomorphic to the symmetric group of \(X\).

Let \(R\) be a ring with 1 and let
\[
0 \to A \xrightarrow{f} B \xrightarrow{g} C \to 0
\]
be a short exact sequence of (left) \(R\)-modules. Show that for any (left) \(R\)-module \(M\), the sequence
\[
0 \to \operatorname{hom}_R(M,A) \xrightarrow{\overline f} \operatorname{hom}_R(M,B) \xrightarrow{\overline g} \operatorname{hom}_R(M,C),
\]
induced by composition of maps, is exact.
Prove the Hilbert Basis Theorem: If \(R\) is a commutative Noetherian ring with identity then so is \(R[x]\).
All parts involve ideals in a commutative ring with identity.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define the terms primary ideal and radical of an ideal.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that the radical of a primary ideal is prime.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Is it true that an ideal is primary iff it is a power of a prime ideal? Answers without an explanation will receive no credit.

problem.text: For this question you may not quote Nakayama’s Lemma. Let \(R\) be a commutative local ring with 1 and \(J\) its unique maximal ideal.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that \(1-j\) is a unit for every \(j \in J\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show that if \(A\) is a finitely generated \(R\)-module such that \(JA=A\), then \(A=0\). (Hint: Consider a generating set for \(A\) of minimal size and derive a contradiction.)

problem.text: Let \(R\) be an integral domain with fraction field \(K\). For \(a \in K\), let \(D(a)=\{r \in R \mid ra \in R\}\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that \(D(a)\) is an ideal and that \(D(a)=R\) iff \(a \in R\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: For each prime ideal \(P\), let \(R_P\) denote the elements of \(K\) which can be represented as a fraction having denominator not in \(P\). Show that \(a \in R_P\) iff \(D(a) \nsubseteq P\).

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Deduce that \(\bigcap_P R_P=R\), where the intersection is taken over the maximal ideals of \(R\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: an integral domain which is not integrally closed in its field of fractions.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: a commutative ring with 1 that is not Noetherian.

problem.text: Classify (up to ring isomorphism) all semisimple rings of order 720.
Let \(C\) be a category and let \((A_i)_{i \in I}\) be a family of objects of \(C\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define what is a product of \((A_i)_{i \in I}\) in \(C\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that any two products of \((A_i)_{i \in I}\) in \(C\) are equivalent in \(C\).
